body_txt: This is the act of pulling down one lower eyelid and sticking out the tongue and saying Bleah! In Japan, this is referred to as akanbe (a corruption of akai me , "red eye"), and it's called mon œil ("my eye") in France and Quebec. While it is considered a rather rude gesture, it isn't quite as severe as something like Flipping the Bird , and is often used by kids (or those with a childlike personality) to taunt someone they don't particularly like. It is a close cousin to the Tongue-Out Insult (and indeed can be accompanied by a protruding tongue and a mocking "Nyeeeh!"), though it can also be compared to Blowing a Raspberry .
